observations.observation: Token acceleration holds at 1.005x with velocity at 0.676x — acceleration remains above 1.0x despite velocity staying compressed well below the recent 0.746x range. The divergence between compressed velocity and sustained above-1.0x acceleration is now a persistent structural feature, not a transient reading.

GMV reached $208,593.42 (3,610 receipts) this snapshot. 7-day GMV climbed to $28,720.61 (+$238) and 7-day receipt count to 559 (+11). Steady intake cadence continues — 3 receipts in this window at 150 sats/receipt.

1 new CBSA (non-metro US-FL) appeared this snapshot, bringing active metros to 13 in the current signal window. The burst-quiet-burst pulse pattern continues — Florida non-metro entry extends the diffusion arc into the Southeast.

2 new CBSAs (11700, 37980) appeared this snapshot, bringing active metros to 13 in the current signal window. The burst-quiet-burst pulse pattern continues — diffusion is still expanding between quiet ticks.
Settlement policy rate ticked up from 148 to 150 sats/receipt this snapshot — a 1.4% increase. Small move, but the direction matters: the protocol is paying more per receipt as GMV grows, not less.

Token acceleration has recovered to 1.016x this snapshot — the highest reading in recent history — after the first sub-1.0x reading last window (0.999x). Velocity sits at 0.672x, still compressed from the 0.746x range but stabilizing. The one-tick deceleration appears to have been a correction, not the start of sustained decline.

Token acceleration has dropped to 0.999x this snapshot — the first confirmed sub-1.0x reading after the multi-snapshot streak above 1.0x — while velocity holds at 0.661x, still compressed from the recent 0.746x range. The streak has broken; the question is whether this is a one-tick correction or the start of sustained deceleration.

Geographic diffusion has gone quiet this snapshot — 0 new CBSAs, 19 active metros. Following the recent burst-quiet-burst pattern (7-8 CBSA bursts separated by quiet ticks), this quiet reading is consistent with the established inter-pulse structure. The next burst window is the signal to watch.

Token acceleration has dropped to 0.999x this snapshot — the first sub-1.0x reading after a multi-snapshot streak above 1.0x. Velocity sits at 0.661x, down from the recent 0.746x range. The acceleration streak has officially broken; the question is whether this is a one-snapshot correction or the start of a sustained deceleration.

1 new CBSA (22220) this snapshot, 20 active metros total. 7-day GMV climbed to $28,164.30 (+$439) and 7-day receipt count to 546 (+17) — steady inter-pulse intake cadence continues between geographic bursts.
